title: The Gig Economy: Balancing Innovation with Worker Rights
topic: The gig economy and worker rights

questions.sentence: The gig economy, characterized by short-term contracts or freelance work rather than permanent jobs, has reshaped the modern labor market.
However, beneath the surface of flexibility and innovation lies a complex debate about worker rights and protections.
They are usually classified as independent contractors, meaning they are not considered employees and therefore fall outside the scope of many labor laws designed to protect conventional workers.
Governments and labor organizations are grappling with how to regulate this evolving sector.
As the gig economy continues to expand, addressing these critical questions about worker rights will be crucial for its sustainable and equitable future.
